articles.title: MONETIZING AI: YOUR PRICING STRATEGY FOR SCALE
articles.summary: As your business grows, your payments and billing infrastructure can become a bottleneck. While some struggle with operational complexity, companies like ElevenLabs and Runway built systems that scale smoothly with user growth [5].

articles.title: THE GREAT CIRCLE OF LIFE
articles.summary: Every major technology era follows the same arc - consumer first, then enterprise, then infrastructure - and AI is no exception. The first wave of winners built directly for consumers, but the pattern from prior eras says the durable businesses will be the ones building tooling and infrastructure layers underneath. If you have seen this cycle before, the playbook is clear: the picks-and-shovels companies outlast the gold rushers. Recognizing where you sit in this arc shapes when to enter, who to build for, and how to time your raise.

articles.title: I ANALYZED ALL YC W26 COMPANIES: HERE'S WHAT IT TELLS US ABOUT THE FUTURE
articles.summary: 85% of YC's W26 batch is AI-first, with 56 of 198 companies building fully autonomous agents explicitly positioned as AI employees targeting every $50-150K knowledge worker role, from AI accountants closing books without human touch to AI law firms staffed entirely by models. Healthcare is the largest vertical with 22 companies going after clinical work itself rather than EHR wrappers, while 13 robotics companies are actively deploying alongside a full picks-and-shovels ecosystem for physical AI.

articles.title: TO THRIVE TODAY, YOU HAVE TO BECOME AN AGENTIC DEPLOYMENT EXPERT. BUT SO, SO FEW ACTUALLY ARE
articles.summary: Companies no longer need engineers to win. They need someone who treats deployment as a core job function - an agentic deployment expert. These hires are worth more than anyone else you can hire right now. They don't need to be technical, they just have to know how to use AI tools to get to deployment. Companies that don't hire an agentic deployment expert risk wasting years of work.

articles.title: THE PSYCHOLOGY BEHIND VIRAL LINKEDIN CONTENT
articles.summary: Posting times, hashtag counts, and emojis have almost nothing to do with LinkedIn virality - emotional architecture does. The gap between posts that explode and posts that die in silence comes down to how the information makes people feel, not what information is being shared. Think identity validation, status signaling, and tribal belonging baked into every sentence. One company went from crickets for 8 months to booking 96 calls in a single month after rebuilding content around emotional psychology instead of information delivery. Spend less time on the insight and more time engineering the feeling.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD A REAL OPERATING PLAN: INSIDE A FULLY LINKED STARTUP FP&A MODEL
articles.summary: Revenue and bank balance stop being enough the moment an investor or board member asks a hard question. A fully linked FP&A model connects revenue forecasts to hiring plans, burn rate, and fundraising timelines in a single living document - change one input and every downstream assumption updates automatically. This is what lets you stress-test scenarios like a churn spike or a delayed round before they actually happen. It is also the kind of financial infrastructure sophisticated investors expect to see when you are defending your raise.

articles.title: HOW TO VET A COFOUNDER
articles.summary: To vet a co-founder, work directly with them on a real project to gauge compatibility and efficiency authentically. Lay the groundwork for collaboration by evaluating past outputs, seeking references, and discussing values to identify potential long-term challenges or alignments. Avoid superficial interviews, and prioritize genuine trials to make informed decisions about meaningful partnerships.

articles.title: YOUR ENTERPRISE CUSTOMERS AREN'T ASKING IF YOU HAVE SECURITY ANYMORE — THEY'RE ASKING HOW IT'S BUILT
articles.summary: Zip Security [13] connects your identity provider, MDM, and EDR into one enforced posture. Automatically. Deploy in two weeks, pass the audit, win the deal. No dedicated security team required. Learn why security is now a sales problem and prepare for the customer audit

articles.title: SUPERMEMORY
articles.summary: Supermemory is an agent memory system that achieves ~99% SOTA accuracy.

articles.title: FRACTAL
articles.summary: Build, test, and deploy ChatGPT apps.

articles.title: THE BESPOKE SOFTWARE REVOLUTION? I'M NOT BUYING IT
articles.summary: Most people don't like computers and only tolerate them because they have to. They likely would rather not think about them at all, given the choice. These people might use AI, but they won't be using the technology to build their own systems and tools to maintain. They just want to get the task done.

articles.title: 1:1S ARE BEING RUN WRONG
articles.summary: Status update 1:1s are a waste of everyone's time, and the fix is simpler than you think. The shift that matters is moving from "what did you do this week" to questions that uncover blockers, ambitions, and candid feedback - the things people will not volunteer in a group setting. A well-run 1:1 builds trust, surfaces problems weeks before they escalate, and develops your team in ways that compound as you scale. Getting this cadence right early creates a management culture that becomes a genuine competitive advantage.

articles.title: VIBECODING IS SELF-EXPRESSION
articles.summary: Vibecoding has quietly turned software creation into a creative act, and that shift puts a large class of consumer utilities on shaky ground.

articles.title: YOUR NOTES ARE THE MOAT
articles.summary: A field report on the convergence happening across personal knowledge tools in March 2026.

articles.title: THE COLD START TRAP: WHY THE BEST SOCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE ALMOST NEVER SUCCEEDS
articles.summary: The "cold start" trap hinders promising social infrastructure by requiring mass initial adoption for value, causing most projects to fail.

articles.title: SCALE IS A TACTIC, NOT A VIRTUE
articles.summary: Silicon Valley treats scale like a moral good when it's actually just a strategic choice.
